issuesFound.fix: Add after line 590: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn:active transform
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have a transform on active state without a reduced-motion override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience nausea from the motion feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 310: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover transform
issuesFound.explanation: Hero CTA button has a transform on hover without a reduced-motion override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience nausea from the motion effect.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 590: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body word-spacing -0.2em
issuesFound.explanation: Body text uses negative word-spacing, which can cause words to run together and reduce readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 150: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: Secondary text colour (#aaaaaa) on white background has 2.3:1 contrast ratio, below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. Users with low vision cannot read this text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: All links have text-decoration: none with no alternative visual differentiator. Users with colour blindness cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 180: text-decoration: underline;

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px, height 28px
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn:active transform

issuesFound.title: productFilter results update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count and grid update dynamically without a live region announcement. Screen reader users are not notified of the content change, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When billing period changes, price updates are visual only without a live region announcement. Screen reader users are not notified of the price changes, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod, after _updateSavingsMessage, add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation marks fields invalid visually but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users cannot perceive invalid state programmatically, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when result.valid is false, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); when true, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (month, quarter, year) do not update aria-pressed when selected. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is currently active, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3980: In the _syncToggleState function, add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', isActive); inside the forEach loop for _periodBtns.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter is applied, the updated product count and empty state are not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the dynamic content change, degrading their experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2900: In the applyFilters function, after _updateEmptyState(totalMatches); add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know whether the menu is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1780: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line 1790: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1886: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 1896: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle button (switching between monthly and annual) does not set aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state. Screen reader users will not know which billing period is selected, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2720: In the setPeriod function, after `_pricingState.period = period;`, add code to set aria-pressed on the toggle button: `if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year');`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results count not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter is applied, the count of products is updated but not announced to screen readers. Screen reader users will not know how many products are displayed, degrading experience.
issuesFound.fix: In the applyFilters function, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add a live region announcement: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied');`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is invalid, the code does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input. Screen reader users may not be alerted to the error state of the field, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120: In the _renderResult function, in the else block (invalid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. In the if block (valid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger does not have aria-expanded initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set on page load. Screen reader users will not know the initial state of the menu (collapsed), degrading experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800: In the mobileNav init function, after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle IconButton has no aria-label prop, making it inaccessible to screen reader users who cannot perceive the icon's function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1039: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>

issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state of the controlled menu to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1055: <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` selector removes the outline (`outline: none`) without providing any alternative visible focus style such as a box‑shadow or colored outline. Keyboard‑only users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); /* add visible focus style */ box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` also removes the outline (`outline: none`) and does not supply any other visible focus cue, leaving focus invisible for mouse users who receive focus via script.
issuesFound.fix: Line 304: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; /* add visible focus style */ box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their outline on `:focus-visible` (`outline: none`) and no alternative visual indicator is defined, making it impossible for keyboard users to locate the focused button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; /* add visible focus style */ box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ toggle buttons strip the outline on focus (`outline: none`) without any replacement cue, preventing users from seeing which FAQ is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; /* add visible focus style */ box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the outline on focus (`outline: none`) and do not supply an alternative visual indicator, hindering keyboard navigation in the footer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950: `.footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); /* add visible focus style */ box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; /* add visible focus style */ box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state on hero images animates `transform` (rotate/scale) with a transition, but the `prefers-reduced-motion` media query does not disable or alter this animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who reduce motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: `.hero-image img:hover { transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg); transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section moves upward on hover using `transform: translateY(-1px)` with a transition, but there is no `prefers-reduced-motion` override to suppress this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 260: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) is used to open/close the mobile navigation drawer, but the script never sets or updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the button to reflect the drawer’s state. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In the `open()` function set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in the `close()` function set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The same hamburger button does not have an `aria‑controls` attribute that points to the navigation drawer element, so assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After obtaining `_drawer`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav-drawer'))`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle (simple on/off switch) missing aria‑pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `data-billing-toggle` acts as a switch (adds/removes a CSS class to indicate state) but never updates `aria‑pressed` to convey its on/off status.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick()` after toggling the visual state, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear))` where `isYear` reflects the current period.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons missing aria‑pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons with `data-period` (e.g., “month”, “year”) receive a visual “active” class via `_syncToggleState()`, but the script does not set `aria‑pressed` on each button to indicate which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState(period)` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive)`, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tabs missing aria‑pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) are given an “active” CSS class in `_syncTabAriaStates()`, but their `aria‑pressed` state is never updated, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the selected filter.
issuesFound.fix: Inside the loop that sets `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive)`, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))`.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing aria‑expanded updates for the suggestion panel
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` (`SELECTORS.searchInput`) opens and closes the suggestion list via `_searchState.open`, but the script never toggles `aria‑expanded` on the input, so assistive technologies cannot know when the list is visible.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open()` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close()` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that open dropdown panels are given `aria‑haspopup` and `aria‑controls`, but the script never sets `aria‑expanded` to reflect whether the panel is currently shown.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance(inst)` set `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance(inst)` set `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑period toggle (on/off switch) missing aria‑pressed updates (duplicate of Issue 3 but for the same element when used as a switch)
issuesFound.explanation: The same element (`data-billing-toggle`) is treated as a switch but lacks `aria‑pressed`.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 3 – ensure `aria‑pressed` is set to “true” when the annual period is active and “false” otherwise.

Simple billing‑period toggle (on/off switch) missing aria‑pressed updates (duplicate of Issue 3 but for the same element when used as a switch)

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module shows and hides the off‑canvas navigation drawer, but it never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger button. aria‑expanded remains undefined (or stale) on page load and after each toggle, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) and ARIA best‑practice rule “All toggle widgets must expose aria‑expanded”.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period switch (month ↔ year) visually indicates its state by adding/removing a CSS class, but it never updates the aria‑pressed state on the toggle element. Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey the current billing period to users, breaching ARIA rule “Toggle buttons must expose aria‑pressed”.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer (`_trigger`) never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is currently expanded or collapsed, which is an A‑level failure.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer does not trap focus when open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, focus can move to any element on the page. A modal‑type navigation drawer must trap focus inside it so keyboard‑only users are not lost in the background content.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack aria‑expanded when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqRegistry.parse` the trigger button receives `aria-controls` but only gets `aria‑expanded="true"` when the item is initially open. Collapsed items have no `aria‑expanded` attribute, leaving screen readers without the required state information.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers missing aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register` the trigger button is given `aria-haspopup` but never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute that reflects the open/closed state of its panel. This violates the disclosure pattern requirements.

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The "contact-first-name" and "contact-last-name" inputs collect personal data (first and last name) but lack the autocomplete attribute. This fails WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose), as users cannot benefit from browser autofill.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 400: `<input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" required autocomplete="given-name">`
  Fix: Line 408: `<input type="text" id="contact-last-name" name="last_name" autocomplete="family-name" required aria-required="true">`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons ("Monthly" and "Annual") control expandable or dynamic content but lack the aria-expanded attribute. This fails WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 710: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed="false" aria-expanded="false">Monthly</button>`
  Fix: Line 711: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed="true" aria-expanded="true">`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` rule (line 1056) removes the default focus outline for mouse clicks, but the `:focus-visible` rule (line 1052) only sets `outline: none` without providing a visible alternative. This removes focus indicators for keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1052: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` selector sets `min-height: 20px` and `padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5)`, resulting in a touch target height below the 24px minimum (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size). This affects users with motor impairments who rely on larger touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships by removing content from the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` selector applies a `transform` animation on hover (lines 1188, 1192) but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block (lines 1196-1199) does not override the `transform` property. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1196: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule (line 260) removes underlines from all links, relying solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color, as users with colour vision deficiencies may not perceive the links.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 260: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`
    Line 261: `a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` selector removes the default focus outline with `outline: none` but does not provide a visible alternative on the base `:focus` state. Keyboard users lose focus visibility when navigating via non-pointing devices.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 400: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`
    Line 404: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text

    Line 492: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` selector sets `min-height: 20px` (line 589), which is below the 24×24 px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) (Level AA). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 589: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` selector has a hover animation (line 1563) and a transform transition (line 1577) that causes spatial movement. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AA).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1563: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`
    Line 1577: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule (line 123) removes underlines from all links, relying solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color (Level A) because users with colour vision deficiencies may not perceive the difference.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 123: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`
    (Alternatively, add a non-colour differentiator like `a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }` or `a { border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }`)

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` selector sets `font-size: 12px` (line 108), which is below the 16px (1rem) minimum recommended for body text readability. Small text can be difficult to read for users with low vision, violating WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text (Level AA).
issuesFound.fix: Line 108: `html { font-size: 16px; }`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `delegate` function (lines 10211–10251) attaches click event listeners to non-native interactive elements (e.g., `<div>`, `<span>`) without providing matching keyboard handlers (Enter/Space). This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) because these elements cannot be activated via keyboard, excluding keyboard-only and screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
For each `delegate` call that targets non-native elements (e.g., `[data-action="select-suggestion"]`, `[data-dropdown]`, `[data-wizard-step]`), add a companion `delegate` call for `keydown` that checks for `Enter` or `Space` and triggers the same action. Example:
    Line 10243: `delegate(listbox, 'keydown', '[data-action="select-suggestion"]', (e, el) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _selectSuggestion(_searchState.suggestions[parseInt(el.closest('[role="option"]').getAttribute('data-index'), 10)]); } });`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.render` function (line 11063) updates the visible suggestion list (`_listEl.innerHTML = ...`) but does not update the adjacent live region (`_statusEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) because screen-reader users are not informed of the number of suggestions available.
issuesFound.fix:
Immediately after `_listEl.innerHTML = ...`, add:
    Line 11067: `_statusEl.textContent = (STRINGS.searchResultsCount || '{n} suggestions available').replace('{n}', String(items.length));`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module (lines 16623–16647) toggle the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) because the button's state is not programmatically exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `open` function, add:
    Line 16631: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
    In the `close` function, add:
    Line 16643: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init`, `dropdownMenu.init`, and `faqAccordion.init` functions do not set `aria-expanded="false"` on their respective trigger elements at page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) because the initial collapsed state is not programmatically exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init`, after `_trigger` is assigned, add:
    Line 16619: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`
    In `dropdownMenu.init`, after registering each dropdown instance, add:
    Line 17019: `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`
    In `faqAccordion.init`, after parsing each FAQ item, add:
    Line 18119: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as a border or box-shadow. This removes the focus indicator for all interactive elements that do not have a specific override, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible border or box-shadow. Users navigating by keyboard will not see which button is focused, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer navigation links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible border or box-shadow. Keyboard users cannot identify the focused link in the footer navigation.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which leaves content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only functionality.

issuesFound.title: Reduced motion media query is empty and does not disable animations
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty. It does not disable the transitions and animations defined elsewhere (e.g., on .btn, .skip-link), which can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, which can significantly degrade readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without sufficient non-color differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. Many links (e.g., in footer, nav) rely solely on color changes to indicate their status, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive color differences.

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24px (and the recommended 44px), making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24px, creating a barrier for touch and pointer users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for search button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] is explicitly sized to 28px by 28px with no padding. This is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24px (and recommended 44px).

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] is explicitly sized to 20px by 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24px, making it difficult to select for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The text color #aaaaaa (var(--colour-text-secondary)) on a white background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 (requires 4.5:1 for normal text).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hints
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint text color #aaaaaa on a white background has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 (requires 4.5:1 for normal text).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on product tagline
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-tagline text color #5588bb on a white background has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.1:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 (requires 4.5:1 for normal text at 14px).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector removes the default outline with "outline: none" but does not provide any visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow, border, or a replacement outline with a color value. This makes it impossible for keyboard-only users to identify which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline with "outline: none" but does not provide any visible alternative focus indicator. Buttons are critical interactive elements, and removing focus visibility without replacement blocks keyboard navigation access.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The header logo link removes outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. This is a primary navigation element, and keyboard users cannot identify when it receives focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. This button controls navigation access, making focus visibility critical for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. Users navigating dropdown menus via keyboard cannot identify which link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. While #search-input:focus has a box-shadow, the :focus-visible variant explicitly removes outline with no replacement, creating inconsistent focus behavior.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify when this interactive button receives focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. These are important navigation elements for user account access.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. These links are primary call-to-action elements within cards.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. These buttons control content filtering and require clear focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. These buttons control billing period selection.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. This region is explicitly made keyboard-focusable for scrolling, so focus visibility is essential.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove outline on focus-visible with only "outline-offset: -3px" specified. No visible focus indicator is provided for these interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. While :focus has a box-shadow, the :focus-visible variant explicitly removes outline with no replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. Checkboxes are critical form controls requiring clear focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses "position: static", "width: auto", "height: auto", "overflow: visible", and "clip: auto" which do NOT hide content visually. This broken implementation means content intended to be screen-reader-only remains visible to sighted users, defeating the purpose of the utility and potentially exposing sensitive UI elements.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has "word-spacing: -0.2em" which compresses space between words. This can significantly impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities, dyslexia, or low vision who rely on clear word separation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 180: body { word-spacing: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element has transform and box-shadow transitions that create spatial movement. While a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block exists at lines 861-866, it is completely empty with no property overrides, meaning the animations continue playing for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element has "transition: transform var(--transition-base)" which creates spatial movement when the link appears on focus. The global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 895 is empty and provides no override for this animation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button has min-height and min-width of 28px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px and significantly below the recommended 44px. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size. This makes the button difficult to activate accurately for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global "a { text-decoration: none; }" rule removes underlines from all links. While some links have hover states with color changes, relying on color alone to distinguish links from surrounding text violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. Users with color vision deficiencies may not be able to identify links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 236: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining a visible alternative (such as outline-color, outline-style, or box-shadow). This renders keyboard focus invisible for all elements that rely on this global rule, blocking keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on button components
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible focus style in this block. It relies on the global :focus-visible rule which is also broken (see Issue 1), making buttons inaccessible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on the mobile navigation button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer links
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple footer link selectors (.footer-brand a, footer nav a, .social-links a) set outline: none on :focus-visible without a visible alternative. Focus is invisible in the footer region.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class is broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Content intended for screen readers only will be visible on the page, cluttering the interface.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in footer brand text
issuesFound.explanation: .footer-brand > p uses #94a3b8 text on a #555555 background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.95:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: .footer-bottom > p uses #64748b text on a #555555 background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.38:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1355: .footer-bottom > p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: #ffffff; margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: .stat-label uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% opacity white) on a coloured gradient background. This results in very low contrast, making the text unreadable for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img is empty. It does not disable the transform transition defined at line 795, causing motion for users who requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion not disabled for reduced motion
issuesFound.explanation: The global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty. It does not disable the transform transition on .skip-link (line 385), causing animation for users who requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for icon button
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-icon has min-width and min-height of 20px. WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 recommends a minimum target size of 24x24px for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for checkbox
issuesFound.explanation: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has width and height of 20px. This is below the recommended 24px minimum target size for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Base font size set below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: html sets font-size: 12px. Since body uses 1rem, the base font size is 12px, which is below the recommended 16px for readability, especially for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: body sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word spacing reduces character separation, significantly impairing readability for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. While some links have hover underlines, the default state relies on color alone to distinguish links from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links rely on color alone
issuesFound.explanation: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a sets text-decoration: none and only changes color on hover. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 as color is the only visual differentiator for the link state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set an initial aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. When the page loads with the mobile menu closed, screen readers have no state information about the trigger. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: Product filter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed when filter state changes
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() function only toggles CSS classes on filter tab buttons but does not update aria-pressed attributes. Filter tabs act as toggle buttons (active/inactive state) and require aria-pressed for screen reader users to know which filter is currently selected. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed when billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState() function only toggles CSS classes on billing period buttons but does not update aria-pressed attributes. These buttons act as a toggle group (monthly/quarterly/annual) and require aria-pressed for screen reader users to know which billing period is currently selected. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: Product count badge updates not announced to screen readers via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._updateCountBadge() function updates the count badge textContent and aria-label when filters are applied, but aria-label changes are not announced by screen readers. Users filtering products receive no audible feedback about how many results match their filter. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).

issuesFound.title: Pricing card price updates not announced to screen readers via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._updatePlanCard() function updates price display elements when the billing period changes, but these updates are not announced via a live region. Screen reader users switching between monthly/annual billing receive no audible confirmation that prices have changed. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the mobileNav module toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now open.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The close function in the mobileNav module hides the navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing Enter/Space keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The _onTriggerKeydown function only handles the Escape key. If the .nav-toggle element is not a native <button> (e.g. a <div>), keyboard users cannot activate it with Enter or Space.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function shows the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies will not reflect the open state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies will not reflect the closed state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is undefined for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _open function displays the suggestion panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input. Screen readers will not announce the list is expanded.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _close does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _close function hides the suggestion panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the combobox input. Screen readers will not announce the list is collapsed.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch mount does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mount function sets up the combobox roles but does not initialise aria-expanded="false". The initial state is undefined for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function expands the FAQ answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies will not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description but fails to remove aria-invalid or set it to "false". The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function updates the visual class of the billing toggle switch but does not update aria-checked (or aria-pressed). Screen reader users will not know which billing period is active.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _updateCountBadge not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function updates the product count text dynamically but does not ensure the element has aria-live="polite". Screen reader users are not notified when the count changes.

issuesFound.title: pricing _updateSavingsMessage not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateSavingsMessage function updates the savings text dynamically but does not ensure the element has aria-live="polite". Screen reader users are not notified when the savings message changes.

fpTitles: mobileNav trigger missing Enter/Space keyboard handler
faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
productFilter _updateCountBadge not announced via live region
pricing _updateSavingsMessage not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: Broken sr-only utility class removes content from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes supposedly screen-reader-only content visible to all users. It fails to use absolute positioning or clipping to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Root font size too small for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, making the root em unit equal to 12 pixels. Since body text inherits this via var(--font-size-base) (1rem), the base font size is 12px, which is below the 16px (1rem) minimum recommended for readable body text and fails WCAG 1.4.8.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing an alternative visual focus indicator such as box-shadow or border-color change. This removes focus indicators for all interactive elements globally, making keyboard navigation impossible for sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline with outline: none but does not define a replacement focus style such as box-shadow or border-color change, leaving buttons without visible focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus indicator, making the mobile menu button difficult to locate for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Search button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes outline: none without adding a replacement visual indicator such as box-shadow or border-color change.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative, making account links inaccessible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector removes outline: none without defining a replacement focus style, preventing keyboard users from seeing which card link has focus.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector removes outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator for the expandable FAQ buttons.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector removes outline: none without adding a replacement, making the scrollable table region difficult to locate for keyboard users who need to scroll horizontally.

issuesFound.title: Form input focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible selectors remove outline: none without ensuring a visible replacement is present (the :focus state has box-shadow but :focus-visible explicitly removes it).

issuesFound.title: Checkbox focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible selector removes outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator for checkboxes.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector removes outline: none without defining a replacement focus style for footer links.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size, creating barriers for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size, making checkboxes difficult to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle class sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended target size, potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu items touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button selectors set min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size without sufficient padding compensation.

issuesFound.title: Submenu links touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size for dropdown menu links.

issuesFound.title: Search button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size for the category filter toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size for the monthly/annual billing toggle.

issuesFound.title: Product CTA button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-body .btn-secondary selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size for product card call-to-action buttons.

issuesFound.title: Social links touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size for footer social media links.

issuesFound.title: Button transform transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line ~840) and has a transform effect on :active (translateY(1px)), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this motion for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Skip link transform transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class has transition: transform var(--transition-base) for the translateY movement, but lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has transition: transform var(--transition-slower) for the 3D perspective rotation effect, and the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line ~1352 is empty, failing to disable the transform animation.

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card transform missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable the motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses word spacing and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, violating WCAG 1.4.8.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces letter spacing and can impair readability for users with low vision or reading disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on statistics labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) against a gradient background of approximately #5588bb, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.44:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter paragraph text
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section > p selector uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8) against a gradient background ranging from #5588bb to #bb88ee, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1 against the lighter purple background, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter form labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section .form-group label selector uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.9) against the gradient background, resulting in approximately 2.9:1 contrast ratio against the blue portion of the gradient, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for text.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed globally without alternative differentiation
issuesFound.explanation: The a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links globally. While some links have hover states with text-decoration: underline, not all link instances are covered, and the global removal without ensuring non-color visual differentiation (such as border-bottom or sufficient contrast difference) violates WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces text spacing below readable levels and interferes with users' ability to override spacing (WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary selector sets background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) with color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.5:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3).

issuesFound.title: Broken sr-only utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which keeps the content in the document flow and fully visible to sighted users while intending to hide it for screen-reader-only use. This contradicts the class purpose and creates visual clutter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 362-372: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a replacement visual indicator such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating the page will not see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345-348: :focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a replacement high-contrast focus indicator. Users in Windows High Contrast mode will lose focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 357-359: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on navigation toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a replacement focus style. The button will not show focus when navigated via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 453: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a replacement visual indicator. Dropdown menu links will not show focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 493: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a replacement focus style.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links

issuesFound.fix: Line 538: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-brand-primary); }

With a font-size of 12px (1rem with 12px base), this fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transition: transform var(--transition-slower) affecting spatial movement, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 588-590 is empty and does not disable this transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on feature cards
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card has transition: transform var(--transition-base) causing spatial movement on hover. The reduced motion override at line 631 only sets transform: none on hover but does not remove the transition property, potentially causing unwanted motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on stat items

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on pricing cards

issuesFound.fix: Line 732: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } }
