issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2550: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes CSS classes and hides the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. This leaves the button in an expanded state for assistive technologies even when the menu is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2570: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init does not initialize aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, it does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button. This means screen readers encounter the button without a defined expanded state until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2590: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function reveals the panel and adds CSS classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the open state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2670: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown close function hides the panel and removes CSS classes but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2690: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a new dropdown menu pair, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger, despite the panel being hidden by default. This leaves the toggle button without a defined initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2650: if (!trigger.getAttribute(ARIA.EXPANDED)) setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation helper links error messages to fields via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid input fields. Screen readers rely on this attribute to identify fields with errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2380: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked as valid, the function removes the error description from aria-describedby but leaves aria-invalid="true" set on the field. This causes screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid after the error is resolved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2390: field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);

issuesFound.title: productFilter._updateCountBadge lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering products, the visible count badge updates to show the number of matching items, but this dynamic change is not announced to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology are not informed that the filter has been applied or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3250: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod lacks live region announcement for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods (monthly/annual), the price values update across multiple plan cards, but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed that prices have changed or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3320: liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

fpTitles: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable but never initializes it with `createFocusTrap()`. When the mobile menu opens, keyboard users can tab outside the drawer and become lost in the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1740 (in init, after _drawer is assigned): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` Line 1750 (in open): Ensure `_trap.activate()` is called (currently guarded by `if (_trap)` which is always false).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, the code sets `aria-controls` and hides the panel, but fails to initialize `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. Screen readers report the button state as undefined rather than collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850 (after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')`): Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The functions that open and close dropdown panels do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger element to reflect the current visibility state of the panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1880 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` Line 1890 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox input missing aria-expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has `role="combobox"` but the `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to indicate whether the suggestion listbox is open or closed. This violates the ARIA combobox pattern and leaves screen reader users unaware of the popup state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2400 (in mount, after setting role): Add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` Line 2500 (in _open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` Line 2510 (in _close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function adds an error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"`. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220 (in markInvalid): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid attribute removal
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state from a field, the function removes the `aria-describedby` reference but fails to set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1230 (in markValid): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons (Monthly/Yearly) act as toggle buttons showing active/inactive states, but the code only toggles CSS classes and does not set `aria-pressed` to communicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150 (inside _periodBtns.forEach): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering products, the visible count badge is updated via textContent, but the change is not announced via a live region, leaving blind users unaware of how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2800 (after setting _countBadge.textContent): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing price changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually with animation, but the new prices are not announced to screen readers, leaving blind users unaware of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100 (after updating plan cards): Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus moves, but the action is not announced via the live region (unlike the keyboard shortcut which does announce), creating inconsistency in the user experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100 (in _onClick, after scroll): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not activate focus trap when opening drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module creates a focus trap variable _trap but never activates it when the drawer opens. Keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer when open, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay to background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2127: Add if (_trap) _trap.activate(); after _lockScroll(); in the open function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu show and hide the dropdown panels but never set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger buttons. Assistive technologies cannot announce the state of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2282: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after inst.open = true;
  Line 2297: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after inst.open = false;

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (when implemented as a non-button element with role="switch") sets role="switch" and tabindex="0" but never updates aria-checked (or aria-pressed) to reflect the on/off state (annual vs monthly).

issuesFound.title: siteSearch open/close does not update aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module's _open and _close functions manage the suggestion listbox visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the search input (combobox) to indicate whether the suggestions are displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2924: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after _searchState.open = true;
  Line 2940: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after _searchState.open = false;

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the _renderResult function calls srUtils.markInvalid which only updates aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input field. Screen readers cannot identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4130: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); after addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation passes, the function does not remove aria-invalid or set it to "false", leaving stale invalid states on fields that have been corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4126: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); after addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger at startup
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function sets up event listeners but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the page loads, leaving the initial state undefined for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2175: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after _initialised = true;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded on triggers at startup
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init function registers dropdown triggers but does not set aria-expanded="false" on them initially, even though the panels are hidden by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2364: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); inside the forEach loop after calling register(trigger, panel);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch does not initialize aria-expanded on combobox at startup
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function configures the input as a combobox but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" before the user interacts with it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3024: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after mounting the listbox;

issuesFound.title: pricing does not initialize aria-pressed on period buttons at startup
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.init function sets the initial billing period but does not initialize aria-pressed on the period toggle buttons to reflect the default selection.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded on triggers at startup

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure toggle button in NavDropdown uses aria-controls to reference the submenu but lacks aria-expanded to communicate whether the submenu is currently open or closed to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion-style section toggle button in MobileNav uses aria-controls but does not have aria-expanded to indicate the visibility state of the submenu to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 529: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ disclosure button uses aria-controls to reference the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded to communicate whether the panel is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1062: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle buttons indicate active state visually via CSS but lack aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technology, violating the requirement that interactive state be programmatically determined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 772: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to each button element.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (Monthly/Annual) indicate selection visually but do not use aria-pressed to convey which billing period is active to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 891: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the Monthly button. Line 898: Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the Annual button.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative chevron character (▲/▼) inside the navigation dropdown trigger is not hidden from assistive technology, causing redundant announcements since the button text already conveys the state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 441: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span containing the chevron character.

issuesFound.title: StarRating star icons missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters (★/☆) inside StarRating are decorative since the parent span provides an aria-label with the rating value, but the star spans are not hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 247: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span mapping the stars: `{filled ? '★' : '☆'}`.

issuesFound.title: Button spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span in the Button component is decorative (accompanied by visually hidden text), but it lacks aria-hidden="true", potentially causing assistive technology to announce the spinner as an unnamed element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 296: Change `<span className="btn-spinner" />` to `<span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />`.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in MobileNav uses the IconButton component without providing the required aria-label prop, resulting in an icon-only button that lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: Add aria-label="Close navigation menu" to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component receives an `invalid` prop to indicate error state but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute, preventing assistive technology from recognizing invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 422: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the input element.

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component receives an `invalid` prop but does not apply aria-invalid to the textarea element, failing to communicate the invalid state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the textarea element.

issuesFound.title: Select missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component receives an `invalid` prop but does not map it to aria-invalid on the select element, leaving assistive technology unaware of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 448: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the select element.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives an `htmlFor` prop intended to associate the label with the input, but the label element does not use the htmlFor attribute, breaking the programmatic label association.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: Change `<label className="form-label">` to `<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">`.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm broken aria-describedby references
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form inputs reference error message IDs (e.g., 'contact-name-error') via aria-describedby, but the FormGroup component is not passed the corresponding errorId prop, so the referenced IDs do not exist in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1218: Add errorId="contact-name-error" to the FormGroup for name. Line 1234: Add errorId="contact-email-error" to FormGroup for email. Line 1250: Add errorId="contact-subject-error" to FormGroup for subject. Line 1266: Add errorId="contact-message-error" to FormGroup for message.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input references 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby, but FormGroup is not provided the errorId prop, so the ID does not exist when the error is rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1165: Add errorId="newsletter-email-error" to the FormGroup for email.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link in DesktopNav has visual styling (nav-link--current) but lacks aria-current="page" to communicate the current page location to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: Add aria-current={item.current ? "page" : undefined} to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link in MobileNav has visual styling for the current page but does not have aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Line 547: Add aria-current={item.current ? "page" : undefined} to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation landmark containing skip links lacks an accessible name, making it difficult for screen reader users to distinguish from other navigation regions on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352: Add aria-label="Skip links" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav landmark missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation landmark does not have an aria-label or aria-labelledby to provide an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 376: Add aria-label="Main" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav__body nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation landmark inside the mobile menu panel lacks an accessible name to distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 507: Add aria-label="Mobile" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The section landmark contains a heading with id="hero-heading" but lacks aria-labelledby to provide an accessible name for the landmark.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby

issuesFound.title: Focus indicators removed without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple selectors use outline: none on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow, border change, or outline with a visible value. Users navigating by keyboard cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA).
issuesFound.fix: Add a visible focus indicator (box-shadow or outline with visible color) to each selector. Example: "Line 247: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }"

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which does NOT hide content visually. A proper sr-only class must use position: absolute, 1px dimensions, overflow: hidden, and clip: rect(0,0,0,0). Content intended to be screen-reader-only will be visible to all users, breaking skip links and other accessibility features.
issuesFound.fix: Line 269-278: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) rule sets outline: none on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. Users in Windows High Contrast Mode cannot see focus indicators, making keyboard navigation impossible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 257-261: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: Interactive elements have touch targets below the 24×24px minimum (WCAG 2.5.8). The .btn-sm class has min-height: 20px, .btn-icon has min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, and checkbox inputs have width: 20px and height: 20px. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 438: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; ... }. Line 449: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }. Line 1350: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Root font-size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) default. This reduces the base size for all rem-based typography, making text harder to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 83: font-size: 16px; (or remove the rule to use browser default)

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces spacing between words. Negative word-spacing can cause words to appear merged and impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 111: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; or change to word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links globally. No alternative visual differentiator (font-weight, border-bottom, background highlight) is provided on the base link. Links in body text are distinguished by color alone, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 143: Remove text-decoration: none; from the base a selector, or add an alternative visual indicator such as border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;

issuesFound.title: Line-height below recommended minimum for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The p element sets line-height: 1, which is below the recommended 1.5 for body text. Insufficient line-height makes text blocks harder to read, particularly for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 152: p { line-height: 1.5; ... }

issuesFound.title: Transform transition without prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has a transform transition, but the associated @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not disable or reduce the motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the transform animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 733-738: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing may impair readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces spacing between characters. Negative letter-spacing can make text harder to read, particularly for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 112: Remove letter-spacing: -0.05em; or change to letter-spacing: normal;

fpTitles: Line-height below recommended minimum for body text

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing any visible alternative focus indicator (no box-shadow, border, or replacement outline). This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements on the page, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 268-271: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class does not hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which does NOT visually hide content. Content meant to be screen-reader-only will be fully visible to all users, defeating the purpose of the utility class and potentially causing layout issues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 289-298: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments or using touch devices may have difficulty activating this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447-451: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which compresses text spacing. Negative spacing reduces readability for users with dyslexia, low vision, or cognitive disabilities, and can cause letters to collide.
issuesFound.fix: Line 229: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without non-color alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links. Links in body text must be distinguishable by more than color alone (WCAG 1.4.1). Without underlines, users with color vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 254: a { text-decoration: underline; } and add: .nav-menu a, .btn, footer a { text-decoration: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, creating motion on interaction. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform transition for the .btn selector. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience transform animations.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 426: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Base font size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which becomes the base for all rem units. This is below the recommended 16px (1rem) default, making all rem-based text smaller than intended and harder to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 227: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 1rem; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Line-height too tight for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The p element has line-height: 1, which is extremely tight for body text. WCAG best practices recommend at least 1.5 for body text to ensure readability. Tight line-height makes it difficult for users with dyslexia or low vision to track lines of text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 251: p { line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); text-wrap: pretty; }

fpTitles: Base font size below recommended minimum

issuesFound.title: Global :focus-visible removes outline without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, which does not provide any visible focus indicator. Outline-offset alone does not render anything visible without an actual outline. This affects all keyboard-focusable elements on the page, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 237: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: .sr-only class does not hide content from visual display
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes content fully visible instead of hiding it visually. This is the opposite of the intended purpose. Screen-reader-only content must be visually hidden but remain accessible to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult() does not set aria-invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the function adds error CSS classes and sets aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5191: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside the else block after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult() does not clear aria-invalid on valid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: When a field passes validation, the function removes error CSS classes but never sets aria-invalid="false" or removes the attribute. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5187: add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` inside the if block after `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function only links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid attribute

issuesFound.fix: Line 2414: add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after the aria-describedby cleanup logic.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not move focus into the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the first focusable element inside the drawer. Keyboard users must manually Tab into the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2861: add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not return focus to the trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the focus order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2883: add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function sets aria-expanded="false" in close() but never sets aria-expanded="true" in open(). Screen readers will not announce the expanded state when a user opens an accordion item, causing confusion about whether content is visible.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function removes CSS classes but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The trigger's expanded state remains inconsistent after closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance() function hides the panel but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The trigger's expanded state remains inconsistent after closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on closed dropdown triggers. Screen readers have no initial state information.

issuesFound.title: Pricing billing toggle does not update aria-pressed on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState() function toggles CSS classes but never sets aria-pressed on the billing period toggle buttons. Toggle buttons must communicate their pressed state to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function calls srUtils.markInvalid() which only links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot programmatically identify invalid fields.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid after being invalid, the code does not remove or set aria-invalid="false". The stale aria-invalid="true" attribute persists, incorrectly indicating an error state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function only links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. This helper is used by form validation and must communicate the invalid state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the aria-describedby reference but never clears aria-invalid. Fields that transition from invalid to valid retain aria-invalid="true", confusing screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation does not initialize aria-expanded on page load

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input has broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput aria-describedby references 'newsletter-email-error' when there is an error, but no element with that id exists in the document. The FormGroup component requires errorId and errorMessage props to render the ErrorMessage, but these are not passed. Screen readers cannot associate the error message with the input.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2060: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>
  Fix: Line 2075: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs have broken aria-describedby references
issuesFound.explanation: The ContactForm TextInput and Textarea components reference error ids (contact-name-error, contact-email-error, contact-subject-error, contact-message-error) via aria-describedby, but these elements do not exist. The FormGroup component requires errorId and errorMessage props to render the ErrorMessage elements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2210: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>
  Fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>
  Fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>
  Fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} hintId="contact-message-hint" errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When isLoading is true, the Button component sets aria-disabled but does not set aria-busy="true". Screen reader users are not informed that the button is in a busy/loading state, which is important for understanding why the button may not respond immediately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 545: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The btn-spinner span inside the loading Button is decorative visual content. Without aria-hidden="true", screen readers may announce the spinner element, creating redundant output alongside the VisuallyHidden loading announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 550: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field asterisk missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk character (*) in the required field marker is decorative visual content that indicates required fields. Without aria-hidden="true", screen readers may announce "asterisk" or "*", creating noise alongside the VisuallyHidden "(required)" text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 598: *

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section contains a heading with id="stats-heading" but the section element itself lacks aria-labelledby. With multiple sections on the page, screen reader users cannot efficiently navigate to this specific section by its accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 975: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection contains a heading with id="hero-heading" but the section element lacks aria-labelledby. With multiple sections on the page, screen reader users cannot efficiently navigate to this specific section by its accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 920: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks navigation component renders a nav element without aria-label or aria-labelledby. With multiple nav elements on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this skip navigation from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 500: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Modal has aria-modal on overlay instead of dialog element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog" (the modal-dialog div). This violates ARIA specification and may cause screen readers to incorrectly announce the modal's boundaries.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2575: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Fix: Line 2578: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: StarRating star characters missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The star characters (★/☆) inside StarRating are decorative visual content. The parent span already has aria-label providing the full accessible name. Without aria-hidden on the inner span, screen readers may announce "star star star star star" redundantly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: {stars.map((filled, i) => ({filled ? '★' : '☆'}))}
